_description: Synthetic canonical UniV2 sandwich on WETH/USDC pool (Uniswap V2 pair 0xb4e16d...). MEV bot buys 5 WETH worth of USDC (front-run), victim buys 2 WETH worth at degraded price, bot sells USDC back for 5.08 WETH (profit: 0.08 WETH ~ $280 @ $3500/ETH). Victim slippage ~ 1.2% (above 0.5% threshold). Expected: sandwich_mev_v1 fires with confidence >= 0.55. Attacker profit_raw > 0 adds +0.15 bonus. Expected confidence = 0.70 (structural + profit bonus). Severity::High.
_source: Synthetic — constructed from Flashbots mev-inspect-py documented sandwich pattern (github.com/flashbots/mev-inspect-py, archived). Real Uniswap V2 WETH/USDC pool address 0xb4e16d0168e52d35cacd2c6185b44281ec28c9dc (Etherscan verified). Token amounts chosen to produce clearly positive profit and measurable slippage. Sprint 21 will replace with captured real sandwich tx data.
_reference: Daian et al. 2019 (Flash Boys 2.0), arXiv:1904.05234; Chi, He, Hu & Wang 2024, arXiv:2405.17944
_adr_note: ADR 0003 carve-out: synthetic fixture from documented attack pattern. No live RPC required.
_notes: Front-run: MEV bot buys 11 USDC (11_000_000_000 raw) with 5 WETH, pushing USDC price up.
Victim: buys USDC at degraded price — receives only 4_100_000_000 USDC (approx 1.2% worse than fair price).
Back-run: MEV bot sells 11 USDC back for 5.08 WETH — profit = 0.08 WETH (80_000_000_000_000_000 raw).
Slippage proxy (no pool state): front_in / (victim_in + front_in) = 5e18 / (2e18 + 5e18) = 0.714 >> 0.5% threshold.
Confidence: base 0.55 + profit_bonus 0.15 = 0.70 (slippage is above threshold so +0.15 too, capped at 0.85).
Wait — all three gates met: 0.55 + 0.15 + 0.15 = 0.85 (capped). Expected confidence = 0.85.
